summary.en: The historic and commercial heart of Jerome, blending Wild West history with an artistic revival and a palpable haunted reputation. Steep streets offer stunning new vistas of the Verde Valley at every turn.

Just off the main thoroughfare, this area reveals the consequences of building a town on a 30-degree slope riddled with mine shafts. The ground itself is a historical artifact of Jerome's geological precarity.
A short walk from the town center, this area is a sprawling, outdoor museum dedicated to the mechanical age. It's a wonderfully cluttered and photogenic landscape of rust and history.

description.en: The world's largest collection of kaleidoscopes, this shop has a steampunk aesthetic and feels more like a gallery of interactive art than a retail store.
A dense collection of artifacts from Jerome's boom-and-bust past, including mining equipment, period photographs, and relics from old saloons and homes.
The streets themselves are a museum, featuring abandoned mining equipment, rusted classic cars, and public art installations that have become part of the landscape.
A solid concrete jailhouse that slid 225 feet from its original location due to dynamite blasts and unstable ground, now resting at an odd angle.
A vast, fascinating collection of classic machines, trucks, and mining equipment built on the site of a gold strike, offering a photogenic landscape of rust and history.

prompt.en: Find the best viewpoint of the Verde Valley from one of the steep side streets and take a panoramic photo.
Take a selfie in the reflection of a historic shop window, trying to capture a 'ghostly' or old-timey feel.
Jerome is filled with art. Find a small, interesting detail on a piece of public art or a gallery sign and take a close-up shot.
Capture a photo of a building that looks like it's straight out of a Wild West movie.
Take a photo that exaggerates the steepness of one of Jerome's streets. Get low to the ground for the best effect!
Find a piece of old mining equipment displayed on the street and take a photo of it with a modern element (like a person or a car) in the background.
Find a spooky-looking alley or corner and take a photo. Bonus points for using a black and white filter.
Photograph a unique or handmade shop sign that you think tells a story about the business inside.
Take a picture of a restored historic building. Try to imagine what it looked like 100 years ago.
Capture an image that shows how buildings are stacked on top of each other on the hillside.
Find an unexpected pop of color on a building, door, or piece of art and make it the focus of your photo.
Find an interesting public staircase and take a photo looking either up or down.
Take a photo that captures the feeling of instability. Use tilted angles to show how the town clings to the hillside.
Find a crumbling wall or a decaying structure and take a close-up shot focusing on the texture of the decay.
Take a selfie in front of an abandoned or ruined building. Try to look like you've just seen a ghost!
Find a spot where plants or trees are growing out of or over an old man-made structure and capture the scene.
Photograph a building or object that is visibly leaning or at an odd angle due to the shifting ground.
Take a photo of a ruin and edit it in black and white to make it look like a historical photograph.
Look for old, crumbling foundations of buildings that no longer exist and take a photo.
Frame a shot with a piece of a ruin in the foreground and the expansive Verde Valley in the background.
Play with light and shadow. Capture a photo of an abandoned structure where the shadows create interesting or spooky shapes.
Find a lone wall or a single piece of a building that is still standing against the odds and make it the hero of your photograph.
Take a photo through a broken window or a hole in a wall of a ruined building (from a safe distance).
Find a retaining wall that is bulging or cracking and take a photo that emphasizes the pressure it's under.
Find the most interesting patch of rust on a vintage vehicle or piece of machinery and take a detailed, close-up photo.
Take a selfie with a row of old trucks or cars in the background. Try to make it look like you've discovered a lost world.
Find a faded or rusted logo on an old piece of equipment or vehicle and capture it.
Take a creative photo focusing on a single cracked or weathered headlight of a vintage vehicle.
Take a wide shot that attempts to capture the overwhelming scale and density of the collection of machines.
If you can see inside a vehicle, take a photo of a weathered steering wheel or dashboard.
Find some exposed gears or engine parts on a piece of mining equipment and take an artistic, detailed shot.
Photograph a piece of weathered wood on an old building or vehicle, focusing on its texture and grain.
Capture the silhouette of an interesting piece of machinery against the sky.
Find a row of similar items (trucks, gas pumps, etc.) and use the repeating patterns to create a compelling photo.
Pose next to a giant piece of mining equipment or a large truck to show the scale difference between a person and the machine.
Find a vehicle with old, cracked, or deflated tires and take a photo that tells a story of a long-finished journey.
